results.title: Taṇhā sutta - Craving

results.title: Craving
results.description: A driving ‘thirst’ that reaches out toward experiences, identities, or outcomes as the place to find satisfaction—“if only I had that.” It spins stories of lack, binds the mind to becoming, and invari

results.title: Taṇhā sutta - Craving
results.description: The Buddha describes the three kinds of craving - 1) craving for sensual pleasures, 2) craving for becoming, and 3) craving for non-becoming.
results.contentSnippet: “Tisso imā, bhikkhave, <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">taṇhā</mark>. Katamā tisso? Kāmataṇhā, bhavataṇhā, vibhavataṇhā— imā kho, bhikkhave, tisso

results.title: Taṇhā vagga - Chapter 24 - Craving
results.description: Dhammapada verses 334–359 depict craving as a binding force, likening it to a creeping vine, a trapped hare, and a spider’s web. The wise cut these bonds, free from passion, aversion, illusion, and lo
results.contentSnippet:
Manujassa pamattacārino,
<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">Taṇhā</mark> vaḍḍhati māluvā viya;
So plavatī hurā huraṁ,
Phalamicchaṁva vanasmi vānaro.

results.title: Taṇhāsaṅkhaya sutta - Extinction of Craving
results.description: Observing Aññāsi Koṇḍañña reviewing his liberation from craving, the Buddha utters an inspired verse praising the arahant who is rootless, unbound, and beyond reproach.
results.contentSnippet: Evaṁ me sutaṁ— ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme. Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā aññāsikoṇḍañño bhagavato avidūre nisinno hoti pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇ

results.title: Taṇhāsaṁyojana sutta - Fettered By Craving
results.description: The Buddha describes the fetter of craving as the most significant bond, bound by which, beings continue wandering on in cyclic existence.
results.contentSnippet: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekasaṁyojanampi samanupassāmi yena saṁyojanena saṁyuttā sattā dīgharattaṁ sandhāvanti saṁsaranti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded"

results.title: Mahā taṇhāsaṅkhaya sutta - The Greater Discourse on the Exhaustion of Craving
results.description: When a misguided monk clings to the idea of an unchanging consciousness that “wanders through rebirths,” the Buddha corrects him, revealing the truth of dependent co-arising. Consciousness, like fire,
results.contentSnippet: Ime ca, bhikkhave, cattāro āhārā kiṁnidānā kiṁsamudayā kiṁjātikā kiṁpabhavā? Ime cattāro āhārā <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">taṇhā</mark>nidānā

results.title: Taṇhuppāda sutta - Uprisings of Craving
results.description: The Buddha describes the four uprisings of craving that can arise in a bhikkhu - 1) for a robe, 2) alms food, 3) lodging, and 4) for this or that state of existence.
results.contentSnippet: “Cattārome, bhikkhave, taṇhuppādā, yattha bhikkhuno <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">taṇhā</mark> uppajjamānā uppajjati. Katame cattāro? Cīvarahetu

results.title: Titthāyatana sutta - Bases Of Sectarian Views
results.description: The Buddha explains the three bases of sectarian views that when closely examined, culminate in inaction. He then shares the Dhamma that is irrefutable, undefiled, blameless, and not disapproved of by
results.contentSnippet: Katamañca, bhikkhave, dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ? Avijjāpaccayā saṅkhārā, saṅkhārapaccayā viññāṇaṁ, viññāṇapaccayā nāmarūpaṁ, nāmarūpapaccayā saḷāyatanaṁ, saḷāyatanapaccayā phasso, phassapaccayā veda

results.title: Bandhana sutta - Bondage
results.description: By what is the world held in bondage? What is its means of locomotion? By the abandoning of what, is every bond cut?
results.contentSnippet:
“Nandīsambandhano loko,
vitakkassa vicāraṇaṁ;
<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">Taṇhāya</mark> vippahānena,
sabbaṁ chindati bandhanan”ti.

results.title: Dhammacakkappavattana sutta - Setting in Motion of the Wheel of Dhamma
results.description: The Buddha’s first discourse to the group of five bhikkhus at the Deer Park in Isipatana, near Varanasi. The discourse explains the Four Noble Truths and the Noble Eightfold Path in brief. It ends wit
results.contentSnippet: Idaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ— yāyaṁ <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">taṇhā</mark> ponobbhavikā nandirāgasahagatā tatratatr

results.title: Paṭhama jana sutta - Gives Rise To (First)
results.description: A deity asks the Buddha what gives rise to a person, what does he have that wanders about, what experiences cyclic existence, and what is this person‘s great fear.
results.contentSnippet:
“<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">Taṇhā</mark> janeti purisaṁ,
cittamassa vidhāvati;
Satto saṁsāramāpādi,
dukkhamassa mahabbhayan”ti.

results.title: Upādāna sutta - Clinging

results.contentSnippet: “Upādāniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu assādānupassino viharato <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">taṇhā</mark> pavaḍḍhati. Taṇhāpaccayā upādānaṁ; upādāna

results.title: Piya vagga - Chapter 16 - Beloved
results.description: DhammaPada verses 209–220 explore the danger of attachment to what is pleasing, how sorrow and fear arise from the beloved, fondness, delight, sensual pleasures, and craving, who is dear to the people
results.contentSnippet:
<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">Taṇhāya</mark> jāyatī soko,
taṇhāya jāyatī bhayaṁ;
Taṇhāya vippamuttassa,
natthi soko kuto bhayaṁ.

results.title: Māgaṇḍiya sutta - To Māgaṇḍiya
results.description: The wanderer Māgaṇḍiya holds the view that sensual indulgence is spiritual growth and harshly criticizes the Buddha for teaching sense restraint, calling him a destroyer of spiritual growth. The Buddh
results.contentSnippet: Seyyathāpi, māgaṇḍiya, kuṭṭhī puriso arugatto pakkagatto kimīhi khajjamāno nakhehi vaṇamukhāni vippatacchamāno aṅgārakāsuyā kāyaṁ paritāpeti. Yathā yathā kho, māgaṇḍiya, asu kuṭṭhī puriso arugatto pak

results.title: Paṭhama bhava sutta - Existence (First)
results.description: For beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving, kamma is the field, consciousness the seed, and craving the moisture for the establishment of their consciousness in the three realms of exist
results.contentSnippet: “Iti kho, ānanda, kammaṁ khettaṁ, viññāṇaṁ bījaṁ, <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">taṇhā</mark> sneho. Avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ

results.title: Samiddhi sutta - With Samiddhi
results.description: A radiant deity tempts young Samiddhi to enjoy sensual pleasures. The Buddha later reveals that true liberation transcends identity and craving, and is only found by those who let go of even the need

results.title: Laṭukikopama sutta - Simile of the Quail
results.description: The Buddha explains how even small attachments can be strong fetters if not relinquished, using similes of a quail and an elephant, and contrasts between the poor and wealthy. He describes four types
results.contentSnippet: seyyathāpi, udāyi, laṭukikā sakuṇikā pūtilatāya bandhanena baddhā tattheva vadhaṁ vā bandhaṁ vā maraṇaṁ vā āgameti. Yo nu kho, udāyi, evaṁ vadeyya: ‘yena sā laṭukikā sakuṇikā pūtilatāya bandhanena bad

results.title: Saccavibhaṅga sutta - Exposition of the Truths

results.contentSnippet: Katamañcāvuso, dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ? Yāyaṁ <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">taṇhā</mark> ponobbhavikā nandīrāgasahagatā tatratatrābhinandinī

results.title: Mahāsatipaṭṭhāna sutta - The Great Discourse on the Establishments of Mindfulness
